# markgrab
> Universal web content extraction — URL to LLM-ready markdown

Python >=3.12 | core: httpx>=0.28, beautifulsoup4>=4.13, markdownify>=0.14
Extras: `[browser]` Playwright | `[youtube]` youtube-transcript-api | `[pdf]` pdfplumber | `[docx]` python-docx | `[mcp]` FastMCP | `[all]`

## `extract()`
```python
async def extract(url, *, engine=None, max_chars=50_000, use_browser=False,
                  stealth=False, timeout=30.0, proxy=None, locale=None,
                  browser_fallback=True) -> ExtractResult
```
| param | type | default | description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| url | str | required | target URL |
| engine | Engine \| None | None | custom engine instance |
| max_chars | int | 50_000 | max chars for text/markdown output |
| use_browser | bool | False | force Playwright rendering |
| stealth | bool | False | anti-bot stealth scripts (browser only) |
| timeout | float | 30.0 | request timeout seconds |
| proxy | str \| None | None | proxy URL (http/socks5) |
| locale | str \| None | None | browser locale, auto-detect from TLD if None |
| browser_fallback | bool | True | fallback to browser on HTTP error or <50 words |
URL routing: YouTube -> YouTubeParser | `.pdf` or `application/pdf` -> PdfParser | `.docx` -> DocxParser | else -> HtmlParser

## `ExtractResult`
```python
@dataclass
class ExtractResult:
    title: str; text: str; markdown: str; word_count: int
    language: str; content_type: str; source_url: str; metadata: dict
```
| field | type | description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| title | str | from og:title > `<title>` > `<h1>` > doc properties |
| text | str | plain text, whitespace-normalized |
| markdown | str | ATX headings, `-` bullets |
| word_count | int | whitespace-delimited word count |
| language | str | `en` \| `ko` \| `ja` \| `zh` |
| content_type | str | `article` \| `video` \| `pdf` \| `document` |
| source_url | str | final URL after redirects |
| metadata | dict | varies by content_type (see below) |
Metadata keys — article: `url`, `domain`, `description`, `image`, `author`, `published` | video: `video_id`, `transcript_language` | pdf: `page_count`, `author`, `subject`, `created` | document: `author`, `created`, `modified`, `subject`

## Engines
`Engine` (ABC): `.fetch(url, *, timeout=30.0) -> FetchResult`
`FetchResult`: dataclass with `html: str`, `status_code: int`, `content_type: str`, `final_url: str`
`HttpEngine(*, proxy=None)` — httpx GET, randomized UA (3 Chrome 131 strings), follows redirects, raises on non-2xx
`BrowserEngine(*, proxy=None, stealth=False, locale=None)` — headless Chromium via Playwright (lazy import), viewport 1920x1080, waits domcontentloaded + best-effort networkidle (max 8s), CloudFlare retry up to 3x with 2s delay if HTML <20KB
Locale auto-detect: `.kr`->ko-KR/Asia/Seoul | `.jp`->ja-JP/Asia/Tokyo | `.cn`->zh-CN/Asia/Shanghai | default en-US/America/New_York

## Parsers
`Parser` (ABC): `.parse(html, url) -> ExtractResult`
`HtmlParser.parse(html, url)` — BS4+markdownify. Pipeline: extract title/meta -> clean_soup() -> find main content (semantic elements > 30+ CSS selectors > body) -> filter_low_density() -> markdownify -> detect_language
`YouTubeParser.parse(video_id, url, title="")` — transcript with timestamps `[MM:SS]`. Lang priority: en, ko, ja, zh-Hans, zh-Hant, de, fr, es, pt, then any available
`PdfParser.parse(data: bytes, url)` — pdfplumber, `## Page N` headings for multi-page
`DocxParser.parse(data: bytes, url)` — python-docx, Heading 1-3 -> `#`-`###`, other headings -> `####`, list styles -> `- ` prefix

## Filters
`clean_soup(soup) -> None` — in-place removal: script/style/noscript/svg/iframe tags, cookie/consent/popup/modal elements, hidden elements (aria-hidden, display:none)
`filter_low_density(content) -> None` — in-place 3-pass: remove aside/nav tags -> remove sidebar class/id patterns (sidebar, related, widget, toc, breadcrumb, social, share, comment, advert, promo, recommend, popular, trending, signup, newsletter, subscribe) -> remove block children with link density >50%
`truncate_result(result, *, max_chars=50_000) -> ExtractResult` — truncate text/markdown at last newline before limit, append `[truncated]`

## Anti-bot
`apply_stealth(context) -> None` — Playwright context init_script: removes navigator.webdriver, sets realistic languages, mocks Chrome plugins (PDF Plugin/Viewer, Native Client), mocks permissions.query, adds window.chrome runtime, spoofs WebGL to Intel Iris

## Utilities
`detect_language(text) -> str` — char frequency on first 2000 chars. Returns `ko` \| `ja` \| `zh` \| `en` (default). Threshold: >50 chars or >10% of sample

## MCP Tools
`extract_url(url, max_chars=50_000, use_browser=False, output_format="markdown") -> str` — single URL, format: `markdown` \| `text` \| `json`
`extract_multiple(urls: list[str], max_chars=30_000) -> str` — sequential batch, returns JSON array of `{url, title, word_count, language, markdown}` or `{url, error}`
Server command: `markgrab-mcp`

## CLI
```
markgrab URL [--max-chars N] [--browser] [--timeout SEC] [--proxy URL] [--format markdown|text|json]
```
| arg | default | description |
|-----|---------|-------------|
| url | required | target URL |
| --max-chars | 50000 | max output chars |
| --browser | off | force Playwright |
| --timeout | 30 | request timeout seconds |
| --proxy | none | proxy URL |
| --format, -f | markdown | output format |
Output: markdown prints `# Title` + HTML comment meta + body | text prints Title/Words/Language/Type header + body | json prints full ExtractResult

## Constants
`USER_AGENTS: list[str]` — 3 Chrome 131 UAs (Linux/Windows/macOS)
`_MIN_WORD_COUNT = 50` — below this triggers browser fallback
`_LINK_DENSITY_THRESHOLD = 0.5` — link ratio above = navigation
`_MIN_BLOCK_TEXT = 25` — minimum text length for density analysis
`_LANG_PRIORITY = ("en","ko","ja","zh-Hans","zh-Hant","de","fr","es","pt")`

## Examples
```python
# Basic extraction
from markgrab import extract
result = await extract("https://example.com/blog/post")
print(result.title, result.language, result.word_count, result.markdown)
```
```python
# Browser + stealth + proxy
from markgrab import extract
result = await extract("https://protected-site.com", use_browser=True,
                       stealth=True, timeout=60.0, max_chars=100_000)
```
```python
# Direct parser usage for local content
from markgrab.parser import HtmlParser, PdfParser
result = HtmlParser().parse("<html><body><h1>Hi</h1><p>World</p></body></html>", "local://")
with open("doc.pdf","rb") as f:
    pdf = PdfParser().parse(f.read(), "file://doc.pdf")
```

[Docs](https://github.com/QuartzUnit/markgrab) | [PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/markgrab/) | [Issues](https://github.com/QuartzUnit/markgrab/issues)
